title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs servemux patterns, precedence, path values, and route safety from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for validated ServeMux route and precedence table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
